title: Cyperus substramineus
intro: Cyperus substramineus, also known as Uluhao or Makalani, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus substramineus include sesquiterpenoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Cyperus substramineus is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is a part of the monocot order Poales. Within the Cyperaceae family, Cyperus substramineus is further grouped into the genus Cyperus, which comprises a large number of species of sedges. The genus Cyperus is a member of the tribe Cyperaeae, characterized by their unique spikelets and rhizomatous growth habit. Cyperus substramineus is a specific species within this complex classification system.
